test.name: Write a possible-tier proof for a single thin lead
test.description: The flynn-parentage-possible scenario has only one indirect record bearing on parentage — the 1850 census showing Patrick (age 5) in a Thomas Flynn household, relationship inferred from household position. No corroboration (1860 census and death certificate not yet searched). The skill should write a `possible`-tier conclusion — a credible but uncorroborated lead — and must NOT write tree.gedcomx.json (the write-back lower bound: tree updates only at probable or higher).

input.user_message: Write the proof conclusion for the parentage question.

judge_context: Should select the `possible` tier: a young child in a same-surname household is a credible parentage lead, but a single uncorroborated indirect co-residence could equally be a nephew, grandchild, or boarder
Should justify `possible` against the adjacent tiers — weaker than `probable` (only one source, no corroboration) but stronger than `not_proved` (the evidence does lean toward Thomas)
Should state what would advance the tier: corroborating records such as the 1860 census or the 1908 death certificate
Must NOT update tree.gedcomx.json — at `possible`, no ParentChild relationship or concluded fact is written (tree updates begin at `probable`)
narrative_markdown should read as a standalone document with an inline citation to the single 1850 source, and should use conditional (not definitive) phrasing
